_meta.method: Cross-referenced Sōtōshū Argentina directory (sotozen.com), Nanzenji — Asociación de Budismo Sōtō Zen de Argentina (nanzenji.com.ar), Templo Zen Shōbōgenji (shobogenji.org / Asociación Zen de América Latina, Kosen Sangha), Asociación Budista Zen Deshimaru Argentina (zen-deshimaru.com.ar), Ermita de Paja (zazen.com.ar), and the budismo.com Argentina directory. Each entry verified against an official lineage organisation or the centre's own site.
_meta.coverageEstimate: High (~85%) for organised Zen sanghas with a website or institutional directory entry. Argentina has the largest Latin American Kosen Sangha presence (20+ dōjōs/groups under Master Stéphane Kosen Thibaut) — only the named, sourced ones are listed below; smaller study groups in towns like Tandil, Saladillo, Reconquista, Lago Puelo, El Bolsón, etc. are confirmed but lack standalone URLs.
knownGaps: Many Kosen Sangha 'Grupo Zen' satellite groups in smaller Argentine towns share the parent zen-deshimaru.com.ar URL and have no separate site/address
Plum Village Buenos Aires sangha exists (visited by Plum Village monastics 2023) but no permanent standalone URL located in 2026
No 'Hokoji' Zen temple located in San Antonio de Areco — the AZI / SZBA reference appears to be confused with the Kobun Chino Hokoji in Taos, NM
No active Kwan Um, Sanbō Zen International, or Ōbaku presence located as of 2026

places.name: Nanzenji — Asociación de Budismo Sōtō Zen de Argentina (Comunidad Zen del Sur)
places.notes: Founded 2005. Argentina's primary Sōtōshū-affiliated zen community (Comunidad Zen del Sur, Asociación Civil).

places.name: Templo Zen Shōbōgenji
places.lineage: Sōtō / Deshimaru (Kosen Sangha — Stéphane Kosen Thibaut)
places.address: Paraje Ojo de Agua, Capilla del Monte, Córdoba
places.notes: Main residential temple of the Asociación Zen de América Latina (largest Zen group in Argentina, founded 1995).

places.name: Dōjō Zen de Buenos Aires — 'Ho Sen'
places.lineage: Sōtō / Deshimaru (Kosen Sangha; Maestro Tai Gen Yamauchi)
places.notes: Urban dōjō of the Kosen Sangha in Buenos Aires.

places.name: Ermita de Paja — Centro de Práctica Zen
places.notes: Founded 1992 by monk Ryūnan Jorge Bustamante; spacious facility for regular zazen and intensive sesshin.

places.name: Zen Dōjō Internacional de Buenos Aires
places.address: Bermejo 6815, Capital Federal, C1408 Buenos Aires
places.notes: Argentina's principal Rinzai practice site; affiliated with Tenryū-ji line. Director: Seizan Feijoo.
